scope_note: QA I Ching Trigram Orbit Cert [285]. Primary sources: (1) Iverson, B. (n.d.). 'Eight Keynotes.' Sympathetic Vibratory Physics articles. www.svpvril.com/svpweb39.html. Accessed 2026-05-30. Establishes 8 I Ching trigrams as 8 QA keynotes. (2) Wilhelm, R. (trans. Baynes, C.F.) (1950). The I Ching or Book of Changes. Princeton University Press. Bollingen Series XIX. ISBN 0-691-09750-X. Standard binary encoding of 8 trigrams. Mechanism: QA Orbit Access Theorem cert [279] (Wall 1960). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): For the 8 I Ching trigrams encoded as 3-bit integers (LSB=bottom line, solid=1, broken=0, Fuxi/Earlier Heaven arrangement): (a) Kun=0 is A1-excluded -- no valid QA state (QA states are in {1,...,N}); (b) exactly 2 of 7 non-zero codes have Satellite access: Dui=3 and Xun=6 (both divisible by 3, neither divisible by 9); (c) no code has Singularity access -- structural impossibility since max code = 7 < 9; (d) the 5 remaining non-zero codes (1=Zhen/Thunder, 2=Kan/Water, 4=Gen/Mountain, 5=Li/Fire, 7=Qian/Heaven) are coprime to 3, Cosmos-only. Verified exhaustively for all 8 codes 0..7. Theorem NT compliance: integer arithmetic on code in {0,...,7}; orbit class by divisibility by 3 and 9; no float feedback into QA layer. Scope: does NOT certify Hz frequency values (floats); does NOT claim orbit partition has consequences for I Ching divination; does NOT claim Iverson's published Hz values (55-163 Hz) correspond to specific trigrams (Iverson did not publish that assignment); does NOT claim result extends beyond {0,...,7}.
